purpose: Machine-readable enum of heavy work classes for the auto-runaway breaker. Replaces the informal 'max 1 heavy work class' rule with a typed, enforceable taxonomy.
see_evidence: Traefik TBAC (Tools/Tasks/Transactions-Based Access Control): access decisions require typed categories
Agentic AI architecture taxonomy: Perception/Brain/Planning/Action/Tool Use — taxonomy IS the control mechanism
ChatGPT CE+SEE verdict 2026-04-26: 'heavy work class gate becomes subjective without a machine-readable enum'

RESEARCH.description: Web search passes, SEE execution, external source gathering
RESEARCH.runaway_risk: high — can expand unboundedly if queries chain

ARCHIVE_EXTRACTION.description: Unzipping, extracting, recursively processing nested archives
ARCHIVE_EXTRACTION.runaway_risk: high — nested ZIPs can trigger recursive expansion chains

CODE_PATCH.description: Writing, modifying, or replacing Python/JS/HTML source files
CODE_PATCH.runaway_risk: high — mutations are hard to reverse; can corrupt active tree

BROWSER_VALIDATION.description: Launching browser, running accessibility checks, Playwright/Chromium automation
BROWSER_VALIDATION.runaway_risk: medium — browser tier can hang indefinitely in sandbox

FULL_EXPORT.description: Creating, re-packaging, or exporting a complete bundle ZIP
FULL_EXPORT.runaway_risk: medium — large ZIPs can cause tool timeouts

REGISTRY_RECONCILIATION.description: Auditing, diffing, or mutating artifact_registry.json or boot_manifest_v1.json
REGISTRY_RECONCILIATION.runaway_risk: high — corrupting registry breaks all subsequent stages

BOOT_RECOVERY.description: Running the boot executor, re-proving recovery, or restoring from baseline
BOOT_RECOVERY.runaway_risk: high — can overwrite active root if unconstrained

PROOF.description: Formal proof stages: SHA chain verification, manifest cross-check, recovery proof
PROOF.runaway_risk: low — read-only; runaway risk is mainly time

enforcement_rule: A stage may contain AT MOST ONE class from this enum. If a proposed stage plan contains two or more, auto-split BEFORE execution. The split boundary is drawn between the two classes. Each sub-stage gets its own P-1 through P7 lifecycle.

pre_stage_classification_block.description: Every stage receipt must declare this block before P0 runs

schema.split_plan: <description of how to split or null>
